title: [Ditto] Battle Guide
summary: An [imposter] [pivot] that copies the oppo[sing] setup sweeper on entry and turns its own boosts against it.
strengths: [imposter] copies Attack, Speed, and all stat boosts on entry
Scarfs past the copied target after factoring item speed
Functions as a universal answer to enemy setup sweepers
weaknesses: All base stats are 48 — any direct hit is extremely dangerous
Useless against non-copyable threats and defensive walls

recommendedMoves.reason: [Ditto]'s only native move — backup for the [imposter] copy
Common target move to mirror back on physical sweepers
Target mirror move against special setup sweepers

recommendedItems.reason: Adds a usable Speed tier on top of the copied stats
Guarantees survives one incoming hit to copy the target

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Ditto] is a 48-across-the-board statline Pokemon with one of the most asymmetric abilities. [imposter] copies the oppo[sing] Pokemon on entry — stats, boosts, moves, ability, and typing — meaning a [sing]le [Ditto] in the back of your team becomes an emergency answer to whichever sweeper the opponent tries to set up. The whole role is one of risk management: you don't use [Ditto] to deal damage, you use it as a hard check that activates whenever the opponent tips their win condition.

## Strengths
The [imposter] ability is the entire pitch. When the oppo[sing] sweeper [dragon-dance]s or [nasty-plot]s and [Ditto] enters right after, the copied stat boosts come along for the ride. Coupled with [choice-scarf], [Ditto] then exists one speed tier above the target and can answer with the same attack it was about to receive. [Ditto] also fills cove[rage] gaps a [normal] team can't solve.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The problem is raw fr[agility]. 48 HP and 48 defenses mean almost any direct hit knocks [Ditto] out before it can copy. [Ditto] is useless against walls and passive setups, and under [trick-room] the strategy inverts, since [Ditto] would inherit the slow target's speed and lose tempo. Opponents aware of [Ditto] also avoid setting up openly, which can waste the slot entirely.

## Recommended Moveset
[Ditto]'s only native move is [transform], which doubles as a backup if [imposter] doesn't trigger. Beyond that, the listed "recommended" moves are really reminders for the copy context: [swords-dance] is the mirror for the oppo[sing] physical sweeper's setup, and [nasty-plot] is the mirror for the special equivalent. In practice [Ditto] always uses the moves it copies, so move slots function as conceptual slots rather than literal builds.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[choice-scarf] is the default item because it preserves [Ditto]'s speed advantage over a copied target, locking in a [sing]le decisive attack. [focus-sash] is the alternate build that prioritizes surviving the swap turn over speed. The ability must be [imposter]. Tera is almost never used, and Tera [Normal] is picked by default just to preserve the typing and avoid conflicts with the copied form.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Ditto] fits teams that force the opponent into setup. Pair [Ditto] with a hazard remover so it isn't chipped to nothing before entering. Avoid stacking [Ditto] with [trick-room] setups, since the two plans are structurally incompatible. A priority abuser on the back line works well to clean whatever [Ditto] leaves behind.

## Play Patterns
Leave [Ditto] on the bench and only enter in response to a confirmed oppo[sing] setup. Once in, use the copied attack. If the copied target has already used [rest] or another [recover]y move, switch out rather than staying in. The [focus-sash] build trades tempo for the guaranteed copy turn, while the scarf build relies on raw speed to win the copy mirror.
